note: Native review confirms the final circular ligand-receptor chord diagram: colored sectors identify cell groups and internal ribbons encode inferred interaction links/weights.

strengths: shows many inferred pairwise links in one overview
risks: dense labels and ribbons occlude weak links
inferred communication is not physical or causal proof

note: Native review confirms a published multipanel embedding reference: the same irregular embedding is colored by cell type, subject and disease, with separate pseudotime-distance panels. It is an appearance/reference anchor, not proof that local code reproduces pseudotime.

channels.color: cell type, subject, disease or pseudotime distance

strengths: compares several annotations on one embedding geometry
risks: pseudotime direction and disease effects cannot be inferred from geometry alone

note: Native review confirms the rectangular pre-polar hiking-data intermediate: category is on x, bar height is sum_length, point position is a second metric and fill is n.

strengths: makes the source mappings visible before polar transformation
risks: source variables are hiking metrics, not pathway/FDR/NES without an explicit adapter

note: Native review confirms the first polar hiking-data intermediate after coord_polar: angle is region, radius is bar magnitude, terminal dot is a second metric and fill is n.

strengths: demonstrates the exact polar encoding chain
risks: source demo is not enrichment analysis
long outer labels compete for space

note: Native review confirms the final grouped correlation scatter: tumor/normal use stable colors, separate fitted lines and displayed N/rho/P annotations; point size maps absolute vertical shift and marginal rugs show distributions.

strengths: shows group-specific association patterns and sample density
risks: reported P values do not establish causality
right legend is close to the crop boundary

note: Native review confirms a final two-panel ROC reproduction: left compares train/test/validation cohorts and right compares countries, with AUC, sensitivity, specificity and accuracy annotations.

risks: AUC does not establish calibration or clinical utility
displayed operating metrics require a declared threshold

note: Native review confirms the final multi-pathway GSEA running-score panel with four enrichment curves, aligned rank-hit ticks and legend annotations for NES, P value and gene count. The article has no target plotting source, so this is visual_only.

visual_fingerprint.layout: single main curve panel with aligned hit tracks and external legend
strengths: shows direction, location and magnitude of enrichment along the ranked list
risks: P values and NES require the underlying ranked-list GSEA computation
overlapping curves can obscure local peaks
